howItWorks.intro: Grout fills the joints between tiles. The amount needed depends on tile size (which determines joint length per square foot), joint width, and tile thickness (joint depth).
rules: Measure your tile dimensions and choose your grout line width — 1/8" is standard for most floor and wall tiles
The grout joint depth equals the tile thickness — thicker tiles need more grout
Order 10-20% extra grout to account for waste, cleanup, and variations in joint depth
howItWorks.outro: This calculator uses the industry-standard formula based on tile perimeter, joint cross-section, and a grout density of approximately 100 lbs per cubic foot. Sanded grout is used for joints 1/8" and wider; unsanded grout for joints under 1/8".

faq.question: How much grout do I need for 100 square feet of tile?
faq.answer: It depends on tile size and joint width. For 12×12 tiles with 1/8" grout lines, you'll need roughly 7-10 lbs of grout for 100 sq ft. For 6×6 tiles with the same joints, about 15-18 lbs. Smaller tiles and wider grout lines require significantly more grout.

faq.question: What's the difference between sanded and unsanded grout?
faq.answer: Sanded grout contains fine sand particles and is used for grout joints 1/8" and wider. The sand adds strength and prevents cracking in wider joints. Unsanded grout is smooth, used for joints under 1/8", and is preferred for polished marble and soft stone tiles that could be scratched by sand.

faq.question: How many square feet does a 25-lb bag of grout cover?
faq.answer: A 25-lb bag of sanded grout typically covers 100-250 sq ft for 12×12 tiles with 1/8" joints, or 50-100 sq ft for 6×6 tiles. Coverage decreases with smaller tiles, wider joints, and thicker tiles. Always check the manufacturer's coverage chart on the bag.

faq.question: Can I mix different bags of grout?
faq.answer: Yes, but always mix bags from the same lot/batch number for color consistency. Mix all the grout you need for a visible area at once if possible. Different production batches can have slight color variations that become noticeable once dried.

faq.question: How long does grout take to dry?
faq.answer: Grout typically needs 24-72 hours to fully cure before exposing it to moisture. Initial set happens in 15-30 minutes. You can usually walk on grouted floors after 24 hours. Wait at least 72 hours before sealing. Full strength is reached in about 28 days.

faq.question: Should I seal my grout?
faq.answer: Yes, sealing grout is highly recommended for all cement-based grout, especially in kitchens, bathrooms, and entryways. Sealer prevents staining, moisture penetration, and mold growth. Apply sealer after grout has cured (7-28 days) and reapply every 1-2 years. Epoxy grout does not need sealing.
